description: The complementizer that introduces embedded yes/no questions, announcing that what follows is an uncertainty being considered, wondered about, or investigated. Together with its required closer 'welo', the pair frames embedded questions the way 'mena'/'meno' frames embedded statements.
sound_symbolism: The word opens with 'we', the glide that reaches outward into possibility, into the unknown. The liquid 'la' flows toward whatever answer might come. Speaking 'wela' feels like opening a door to uncertainty, inviting a question to unfold before resolving it.
grammatical_notes: Introduces embedded yes/no questions. Always paired with closer 'welo'. Structure: [Subject] wela [embedded question] welo [main verb]. Example: 'mia wela shia so shua welo phaelo' (I wonder whether they will arrive). Distinguished from 'wa' (direct question particle) which marks questions expecting an answer; 'wela'/'welo' embeds questions as objects of verbs like 'wonder', 'ask', 'know'.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: The word acknowledges that not-knowing is a valid state. Embedded questions honor uncertainty rather than rushing past it, supporting the practice of holding questions openly.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Embedding questions rather than demanding answers supports non-coercive communication. 'Wela' allows speakers to express uncertainty without forcing resolution, creating space for genuine inquiry.
